decks.notes: This deck revolves around utilizing a mix of spells, creatures, and lands to consistently apply pressure on the opponent. It aims to quickly deploy threats like Marauding Mako and Vivi Ornitier while supporting them with powerful instant and sorcery spells like Torch the Tower and Into the Flood Maw. The deck also includes key cards like Winternight Stories and Profit's Eidetic Memory to maintain card advantage and control the game. By efficiently utilizing its resources and maintaining a proactive game plan, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents and secure victories.

This deck focuses on utilizing a mix of removal spells, powerful creatures, and disruption to control the game and outvalue opponents. With cards like Fatal Push, Abrupt Decay, and Thoughtseize to handle threats, as well as creatures like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to pressure the opponent, the deck aims to grind out victories by controlling the board and hand advantage. The inclusion of utility lands like Mutavault and Boseiju, Who Endures provide additional flexibility and power to the deck's game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing spells to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It aims to stall the game until it can establish a strong advantage and eventually win through card advantage and incremental value. The deck also includes a few creatures for potential pressure and beatdown, but the main focus is on controlling the game and outlasting the opponent's threats. It leverages a mix of burn spells, counterspells, and card draw to maintain control and dictate the pace of the game.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to maintain a fast and aggressive playstyle by utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to deal damage quickly to the opponent. The deck focuses on tempo advantage, using cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Sleight of Hand to maintain card advantage while putting pressure on the opponent's life total. Cards like Reckless Rage and Burst Lightning provide removal options while creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage offer consistent damage output. The deck aims to overload the opponent with threats and finish the game before they can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to consistently bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard by casting cheap spells such as Consider, Fiery Impulse, and Lightning Axe. The deck is designed to be fast-paced and aggressive, utilizing cards like Picklock Prankster and Ledger Shredder to disrupt the opponent's strategy. It also includes card draw and recursion with Treasure Cruise and Proft's Eidetic Memory. With a mix of control elements like Spell Pierce and Brazen Borrower, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly with the powerful flying Phoenix.

The deck focuses on utilizing cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can overwhelm opponents. It also includes various support cards like Bitter Triumph and Cache Grab to maintain control and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck has a mix of creatures, lands, and spells to provide versatility and a strong board presence. Additionally, cards like Multiversal Passage and Thundering Broodwagon help ramp up resources and mana for bigger plays. The overall strategy is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and outpace the opponent for a swift victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing disruptive spells like Duress and Thoughtseize to control the opponent's hand while using removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push to deal with threats on the battlefield. The creatures in the deck offer additional control elements, with cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Graveyard Trespasser providing powerful recursion and graveyard manipulation. The lands in the deck support the strategy by providing mana fixing and utility. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and outvalue them in a slow and grindy matchup.

The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly and aggressively play cheap and efficient spells and creatures to overwhelm the opponent. The deck focuses on utilizing prowess triggers and instant/sorcery spells to deal damage to the opponent while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Boomerang Basics and Reckless Rage. By maintaining a low mana curve and a high number of threats, this deck aims to apply constant pressure and win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly equip creatures with powerful artifacts and equipment to increase their power and aggression on the battlefield. By utilizing cards like Colossus Hammer, Leyline Axe, and Samurai's Katana, along with creatures like Adanto Vanguard and Fervent Champion, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with fast, strong attacks. Additionally, cards like Sheltered by Ghosts and Kemba, Kha Enduring provide protection and support for the aggressive strategy. The mana base includes a mix of lands to support the aggressive Boros color combination.

The main strategy of this deck revolves around assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate infinite tokens and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes various support cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Thoughtseize to control the game and protect the combo pieces. It also has cards like Esika's Chariot and Overlord of the Balemurk to provide additional threats and flexibility in gameplay. The mana base is carefully crafted to ensure consistency and enable the deck to execute its game plan efficiently.

The key concept of this Dimir Control deck is to utilize a companion, Yorion, Sky Nomad, to create a 80-card deck and take advantage of its ability. The deck focuses on disruption and removal spells such as Fatal Push and Thoughtseize, while also incorporating powerful control cards like Boomerang Basics and Hopeless Nightmare. The land base is carefully crafted to ensure consistency in mana sources. The goal is to control the game through removal, disruption, and card advantage provided by Yorion, ultimately overwhelming the opponent and securing victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana quickly, allowing for powerful combos and synergies to be activated. By ramping up mana through various land cards and creatures, the player is able to cast spells like Scapeshift and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods to overwhelm their opponent. The deck also includes support cards like The Wandering Minstrel and Aftermath Analyst to further enhance its combo potential and maintain control over the game. Overall, the key strategy is to accelerate mana production and execute game-winning combos efficiently.

This deck focuses on using the Greasefang Parhelion combo to generate value and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards that support the combo such as Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance, as well as protection cards like Duress and Thoughtseize. The deck aims to control the board with Iron-Shield Elf and Guardian of New Benalia while setting up the combo to create powerful and difficult-to-answer threats. The mana base includes lands that support the strategy, such as Bleachbone Verge and The Mycosynth Gardens.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on aggressive plays and tempo advantage by utilizing a mix of efficient creatures and powerful spells to quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck also includes cards that provide card advantage and flexibility in different situations, allowing the player to adapt to various board states. With a good mix of card draw, removal, and threats, this deck aims to put pressure on the opponent and close out games quickly before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the combo between Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. The deck aims to control the game with cards like Thoughtseize and Cache Grab, while also providing protection with creatures like Selfless Savior. Additionally, the deck includes ramp cards like Multiversal Passage and Formidable Speaker to accelerate the combo. Overall, the deck focuses on setting up the combo pieces efficiently and quickly to dominate the game.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively disrupt the opponent's strategy while applying pressure through evasive threats. By utilizing a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and powerful creatures with abilities that provide additional advantage, the deck aims to control the game tempo and outvalue the opponent. With a mix of control elements and aggressive threats, this Dimir Aggro deck aims to close out games quickly while keeping the opponent off balance.
